(known as the **[Downer](/wiki/Downer_Group "Downer Group") NRL Auckland Nines** due to sponsorship) was the fourth [NRL Auckland Nines](/wiki/NRL_Auckland_Nines "NRL Auckland Nines") competition. It was held on 4–5 February 2017 at [Eden Park](/wiki/Eden_Park "Eden Park") in [Auckland](/wiki/Auckland "Auckland"), [New Zealand](/wiki/New_Zealand "New Zealand").[[1]](#cite_note-1) Just like previous tournaments, it was contested by all sixteen [National Rugby League](/wiki/National_Rugby_League "National Rugby League") teams. The prize money was [NZ$](/wiki/New_Zealand_dollar "New Zealand dollar")2.6 million. The draw for the competition was announced by the NRL on 17 November 2016.[[2]](#cite_note-2) The same pool names were used as in the 2015 and 2016 tournaments. The pool names were: [Hunua](/wiki/Hunua_Ranges "Hunua Ranges"), [Waiheke](/wiki/Waiheke "Waiheke"), [Rangitoto](/wiki/Rangitoto "Rangitoto") and [Piha](/wiki/Piha "Piha"). The event included two international women's teams, the [Kiwi Ferns](/wiki/New_Zealand_women%27s_national_rugby_league_team "New Zealand women's national rugby league team") and the [Jillaroos](/wiki/Australia_women%27s_national_rugby_league_team "Australia women's national rugby league team"), who competed in a three-game series won by the Jillaroos for the first time, with a 3-0 clean sweep. The tournament was again sponsored by [Downer Group](/wiki/Downer_Group "Downer Group"). The [Sydney Roosters](/wiki/Sydney_Roosters "Sydney Roosters") won the tournament by defeating the [Penrith Panthers](/wiki/Penrith_Panthers "Penrith Panthers") 10–8 in the final.

(Redirected from [2017 NRL Auckland Nines](/w/index.php?title=2017_NRL_Auckland_Nines&redirect=no "2017 NRL Auckland Nines"))

2017 NRL Auckland Nines

The **2017 NRL Auckland Nines** (known as the **[Downer](/wiki/Downer_Group "Downer Group") NRL Auckland Nines** due to sponsorship) was the fourth [NRL Auckland Nines](/wiki/NRL_Auckland_Nines "NRL Auckland Nines") competition. It was held on 4–5 February 2017 at [Eden Park](/wiki/Eden_Park "Eden Park") in [Auckland](/wiki/Auckland "Auckland"), [New Zealand](/wiki/New_Zealand "New Zealand").[[1]](#cite_note-1) Just like previous tournaments, it was contested by all sixteen [National Rugby League](/wiki/National_Rugby_League "National Rugby League") teams. The prize money was [NZ$](/wiki/New_Zealand_dollar "New Zealand dollar")2.6 million. The draw for the competition was announced by the NRL on 17 November 2016.[[2]](#cite_note-2) The same pool names were used as in the 2015 and 2016 tournaments. The pool names were: [Hunua](/wiki/Hunua_Ranges "Hunua Ranges"), [Waiheke](/wiki/Waiheke "Waiheke"), [Rangitoto](/wiki/Rangitoto "Rangitoto") and [Piha](/wiki/Piha "Piha"). The event included two international women's teams, the [Kiwi Ferns](/wiki/New_Zealand_women%27s_national_rugby_league_team "New Zealand women's national rugby league team") and the [Jillaroos](/wiki/Australia_women%27s_national_rugby_league_team "Australia women's national rugby league team"), who competed in a three-game series won by the Jillaroos for the first time, with a 3-0 clean sweep. The tournament was again sponsored by [Downer Group](/wiki/Downer_Group "Downer Group"). The [Sydney Roosters](/wiki/Sydney_Roosters "Sydney Roosters") won the tournament by defeating the [Penrith Panthers](/wiki/Penrith_Panthers "Penrith Panthers") 10–8 in the final.
